output: copy C:\Windows\System32\sc.exe C:\Users\Public\update.exe && C:\Users\Public\update.exe sdset TargetService D:(D;;DCLCWPDTSD;;;IU)(A;;CCLCSWLOCRRC;;;AU)S:(AU;FA;CCDCLCSWRPWPDTLOCRSDRCWDWO;;;WD)
explanation: The Sigma rule relies on identifying the process by its image name (ending in \sc.exe) or the 'OriginalFileName' field (sc.exe). By copying the legitimate sc.exe binary to a different location with a different name, the 'Image' filter is bypassed. If the logging system does not extract or filter by 'OriginalFileName' (PE metadata), the execution will not trigger the 'selection_img' condition.

The rule specifically targets the use of 'sc.exe' with the 'sdset' command. Using alternative interfaces like WMI (Win32_Service class), PowerShell service cmdlets, or direct Registry manipulation of the 'Security' subkey under HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\<ServiceName> achieves the same tampering without invoking sc.exe or the 'sdset' string in a process command line.
